generatedSentence: Jones, known for his unique collection of eighty antique refrigerators numbering his hammock in the outback, often suffered from hiccups after thickly buttered flapjack. He cajoled a pet gecko named Bumper to apprise him of any intel hailing from the online world, as he was immune to direct digital contact. His legacy was built on lawful acquisition, yet he sensed an insanely marketed violation of peace was imminent, requiring new tactics to escape the encroaching influence, perhaps even levying an old djinn if necessary. The outer world opened with a daymare when a lookalike vehicle, emblazoned with a chevron, carried agents of the regime, their faces obscured by elaborate makeup and a single freckle painted on each. Their leader, Jones's opponent, was eager to enjoined all resistance, promoting a magical serum via an online parody of poetry, claiming it offered an injection of peace. Jones babbled about the injustice, feeling a strange itchy blemish on his arm; the serum was a new form of stock control, forcing namaste from its unwilling subjects, replacing his wishful thinking with a rising mood of dread. The ambush was swift; firefighters in baggy flak jackets, corrupted by the serum, tried to identify him, forcing Jones to duck behind a railroad car, his heart doing an upthrow. His pockets held a jeweled longbow, an ancient instrument for which he had a knack, but the opponent also wielded a gunlock launcher, firing lazy launchers into the air, creating a nightmare overlay of energy. An acolyte, seeing Jones's mackintosh and thinking him a mere worker for their cause, offered a piece of papaya and prune, unaware Jones was skimmed past them, his face covered by a mackinaw, escaping their efforts to block his path. Jones, reading the situation, realized his small account of earnings affords little protection against these vikings of technology. He utilized his knowledge of the hills for cover, recognizing the ragweed fields as an asylum from the advancing forces. A newcomer onlooker, witnessing the everyday efforts of the regime to control, found Jones’s jawbreaker defiance helpful. With a joypad in hand, Jones knew he had to jones for total victory, ensuring the regime's control vanished into the realm of a true dream, not their engineered daymare, using his last effort to turn the tide.
panelSentences: Jones, known for his unique collection of eighty antique refrigerators numbering his hammock in the outback, often suffered from hiccups after thickly buttered flapjack. He cajoled a pet gecko named Bumper to apprise him of any intel hailing from the online world, as he was immune to direct digital contact. His legacy was built on lawful acquisition, yet he sensed an insanely marketed violation of peace was imminent, requiring new tactics to escape the encroaching influence, perhaps even levying an old djinn if necessary.

The outer world opened with a daymare when a lookalike vehicle, emblazoned with a chevron, carried agents of the regime, their faces obscured by elaborate makeup and a single freckle painted on each. Their leader, Jones's opponent, was eager to enjoined all resistance, promoting a magical serum via an online parody of poetry, claiming it offered an injection of peace. Jones babbled about the injustice, feeling a strange itchy blemish on his arm; the serum was a new form of stock control, forcing namaste from its unwilling subjects, replacing his wishful thinking with a rising mood of dread.

The ambush was swift; firefighters in baggy flak jackets, corrupted by the serum, tried to identify him, forcing Jones to duck behind a railroad car, his heart doing an upthrow. His pockets held a jeweled longbow, an ancient instrument for which he had a knack, but the opponent also wielded a gunlock launcher, firing lazy launchers into the air, creating a nightmare overlay of energy. An acolyte, seeing Jones's mackintosh and thinking him a mere worker for their cause, offered a piece of papaya and prune, unaware Jones was skimmed past them, his face covered by a mackinaw, escaping their efforts to block his path.

Jones, reading the situation, realized his small account of earnings affords little protection against these vikings of technology. He utilized his knowledge of the hills for cover, recognizing the ragweed fields as an asylum from the advancing forces. A newcomer onlooker, witnessing the everyday efforts of the regime to control, found Jones’s jawbreaker defiance helpful. With a joypad in hand, Jones knew he had to jones for total victory, ensuring the regime's control vanished into the realm of a true dream, not their engineered daymare, using his last effort to turn the tide.
rawPanelSentences: Jones, known for his **unique** collection of **eighty** antique **refrigerators** **numbering** his **hammock** in the **outback**, often suffered from **hiccups** after **thickly** buttered **flapjack**. He **cajoled** a pet **gecko** named Bumper to **apprise** him of any **intel** **hailing** from the **online** world, as he was **immune** to direct digital contact. His **legacy** was built on **lawful** acquisition, yet he sensed an **insanely** **marketed** **violation** of peace was imminent, requiring new **tactics** to **escape** the encroaching influence, perhaps even **levying** an old **djinn** if necessary.

The **outer** world **opened** with a **daymare** when a **lookalike** **vehicle**, emblazoned with a **chevron**, carried **agents** of the regime, their faces obscured by elaborate **makeup** and a single **freckle** painted on each. Their leader, Jones's **opponent**, was **eager** to **enjoined** all **resistance**, promoting a **magical** serum via an **online** **parody** of **poetry**, claiming it offered an **injection** of peace. Jones **babbled** about the **injustice**, feeling a strange **itchy** **blemish** on his arm; the serum was a new form of **stock** control, forcing **namaste** from its unwilling **subjects**, replacing his **wishful** thinking with a rising **mood** of dread.

The **ambush** was swift; **firefighters** in **baggy** **flak jacket**s, corrupted by the serum, tried to **identify** him, forcing Jones to **duck** behind a **railroad** car, his heart doing an **upthrow**. His **pockets** held a **jeweled** **longbow**, an ancient **instrument** for which he had a **knack**, but the **opponent** also wielded a **gunlock** launcher, firing **lazy** **launchers** into the air, creating a **nightmare** **overlay** of energy. An **acolyte**, seeing Jones's **mackintosh** and thinking him a mere **worker** for their cause, offered a piece of **papaya** and **prune**, unaware Jones was **skimmed** past them, his face covered by a **mackinaw**, escaping their **efforts** to **block** his path.

Jones, **reading** the situation, realized his small **account** of **earnings** **affords** little protection against these **vikings** of technology. He utilized his knowledge of the **hills** for cover, recognizing the **ragweed** fields as an **asylum** from the advancing forces. A **newcomer** **onlooker**, witnessing the **everyday** **efforts** of the regime to control, found Jones’s **jawbreaker** defiance **helpful**. With a **joypad** in hand, Jones knew he had to **jones** for total victory, ensuring the regime's control **vanished** into the realm of a true **dream**, not their engineered **daymare**, using his last **effort** to turn the tide.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. PROTAGONIST: A solitary figure with a weathered face, wearing a patched olive-green mackintosh and a worn leather flak jacket. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Jones, looking over a collection of eclectic antique refrigerators in a dimly lit, high-tech outback shack, adjusting a hammock, with a small gecko perched on his shoulder, seen from a wide-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An imposing, chrome-plated vehicle with a glowing chevron logo arrives at the shack's entrance, two masked agents with identical freckles emerging, viewed from a low-angle shot to emphasize their menace. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Jones, in his mackintosh, ducks behind a rusty railroad car, expertly wielding a jeweled longbow, while explosive projectiles launch from off-panel, captured in a dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Jones stands triumphantly on a windswept hill covered in ragweed, a glowing joypad held aloft, silhouetted against a futuristic cityscape, depicted in a heroic long shot.

definitions.definition: Of hail, to fall from the sky.

To greet; give salutation to; salute.

definitions.definition: To call out loudly in order to gain the attention of
definitions.example: Hail a taxi.

The act by which somebody is hailed.
In a thick manner.
To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.

definitions.definition: To sell

definitions.definition: To deal in a market; to buy or sell; to make bargains for provisions or goods.
The act or an instance of violating or the condition of being violated.
To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To lower (something) into water; to thrust or plunge under liquid and suddenly withdraw.
To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.

definitions.example: I'm just going to duck into the loo for a minute, can you hold my bag?

definitions.definition: To notify, or to make aware; to inform.
Intelligence (secret information)
definitions.example: Intel is important in espionage.

definitions.definition: In an insane manner; not sanely.
To a great degree; very much.
definitions.example: Disney has some insanely fun roller coasters.

definitions.definition: Any lizard of the family Gekkonidae. They are small, carnivorous, mostly nocturnal animals with large eyes and adhesive toes enabling them to climb on vertical and upside-down surfaces.
To move in the manner of a gecko; to attach to a vertical or upside-down surface.
To persuade someone to do something which they are reluctant to do, especially by flattery or promises; to coax.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
definitions.example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definitions.definition: Of a feature, such that only one holder has it.
Particular, characteristic.
Of a rare quality, unusual.
Someone or something that bumps.
A drinking vessel filled to the brim.
Anything large or successful.
Parts at the front and back of a vehicle which are meant to absorb the impact of a collision; fender.

definitions.example: The company sells screw-on rubber bumpers and feet.

definitions.definition: A bouncer.
A side wall of a pool table.
A short ditty or jingle used to separate a show from the advertisements.
A covered house at a theatre, etc., in honour of some favourite performer.
A woman's posterior, particularly one that is considered full and desirable.

An extra musician (not notated in the score) who assists the principal French horn by playing less-exposed passages, so that the principal can save their 'lip' for difficult solos. Also applied to other sections of the orchestra.
An object on a playfield that applies force to the pinball when hit, often giving a minor increase in score.
To drink from the vessels called bumpers.
Large; filled to the bumpers at the top of a silo.
definitions.example: We harvested a bumper crop of arugula and parsnips this year.

definitions.definition: Heroin.
An addiction or intense craving.
definitions.example: I’ve got a basketball jones!

definitions.definition: Have an intense craving.
definitions.example: I’m jonesing for some basketball.

definitions.definition: The cardinal number occurring after seventy-nine and before eighty-one, represented in Roman numerals as LXXX and in Arabic numerals as 80.
The act of leaving a dangerous or unpleasant situation.

definitions.definition: Leakage or outflow, as of steam or a liquid, or an electric current through defective insulation.

A holiday, viewed as time away from the vicissitudes of life.
Escape key

definitions.definition: To elude the observation or notice of; to not be seen or remembered by.
definitions.example: The name of the hotel escapes me at present.

definitions.definition: To cause (a single character, or all such characters in a string) to be interpreted literally, instead of with any special meaning it would usually have in the same context, often by prefixing with another character.
definitions.example: Brion escaped the double quote character on Windows by adding a second double quote within the literal.

definitions.definition: To halt a program or command by pressing a key (such as the "Esc" key) or combination of keys.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.

To operate a railroad.

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.

definitions.definition: A force acting on a body in the direction of its motion.

To look at and interpret letters or other information that is written.

definitions.definition: To read work(s) written by (a named author).

definitions.example: I can read his feelings in his face.

definitions.definition: To consist of certain text.

definitions.definition: To substitute (a corrected piece of text in place of an erroneous one); used to introduce an emendation of a text.

definitions.definition: To observe and comprehend (a displayed signal)

definitions.definition: To fetch data from (a storage medium, etc.).
definitions.example: to read a hard disk; to read a port; to read the keyboard

definitions.definition: To think, believe; to consider (that).
To advise; to counsel. See rede.
To tell; to declare; to recite.
To recognise (someone) as being transgender.
definitions.example: Every time I go outside, I worry that someone will read me.

definitions.definition: To call attention to the flaws of (someone) in either a playful, a taunting, or an insulting way.

synonyms: read aloud, read out, read out loud, speak

definitions.definition: The process of interpreting written language.
The process of interpreting a symbol, a sign or a measuring device.

definitions.definition: An event at which written material is read aloud.
definitions.example: a poetry reading.

definitions.definition: An interpretation.
definitions.example: a reading of the current situation.

definitions.definition: Something to read; reading material.

definitions.example: He's a man of good reading.

definitions.definition: (legislature) One of several stages a bill passes through before becoming law.
A piece of literature or passage of scripture read aloud to an audience: readings from the Bible
The traditional greeting when saying the word namaste, with folded hands and a slight bow.
In yoga, the pose associated with this word, usually with the flat hands held palms together, fingers up, in front of the heart and a slight bow.
To utter "namaste".

definitions.definition: Literally "I bow to you"; used as a greeting or acknowledgement of the equality of all, which pays honor to the sacredness of all.
An item's composition.
definitions.example: To understand how a nuclear reactor works, we must first look at its makeup.

definitions.definition: Cosmetics; colorants and other substances applied to the skin to alter its appearance.
definitions.example: She is wearing a lot of makeup.

definitions.definition: Replacement; material used to make up for the amount that has been used up.
A test given to students allowing them to repeat failed material.
Of or relating to magic.
Enchanting.
definitions.example: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

definitions.definition: A store or supply.
The capital raised by a company through the issue of shares. The total of shares held by an individual shareholder.
The raw material from which things are made; feedstock.
Stock theater, summer stock theater.
The trunk and woody main stems of a tree. The base from which something grows or branches.
Any of the several species of cruciferous flowers in the genus Matthiola.
A handle or stem to which the working part of an implement or weapon is attached.
Part of a machine that supports items or holds them in place.
A bar, stick or rod.
A type of (now formal or official) neckwear.
A bed for infants; a crib, cot, or cradle
A piece of wood magically made to be just like a real baby and substituted for it by magical beings.
A cover for the legs; a stocking.
A block of wood; something fixed and solid; a pillar; a firm support; a post.
(by extension) A person who is as dull and lifeless as a stock or post; one who has little sense.
The longest part of a split tally stick formerly struck in the exchequer, which was delivered to the person who had lent the king money on account, as the evidence of indebtedness.
(in the plural) The frame or timbers on which a ship rests during construction.
(in the plural) Red and grey bricks, used for the exterior of walls and the front of buildings.
In tectology, an aggregate or colony of individuals, such as as trees, chains of salpae, etc.
The beater of a fulling mill.
To have on hand for sale.

definitions.definition: To provide with material requisites; to store; to fill; to supply.

definitions.definition: To allow (cows) to retain milk for twenty-four hours or more prior to sale.
To put in the stocks as punishment.
To fit (an anchor) with a stock, or to fasten the stock firmly in place.
To arrange cards in a certain manner for cheating purposes; to stack the deck.
Of a type normally available for purchase/in stock.

definitions.definition: (racing, of a race car) Having the same configuration as cars sold to the non-racing public, or having been modified from such a car.
Straightforward, ordinary, just another, very basic.

definitions.definition: To pass lightly; to glide along in an even, smooth course; to glide along near the surface.
To pass near the surface of; to brush the surface of; to glide swiftly along the surface of.
To hasten along with superficial attention.
To put on a finishing coat of plaster.
To throw an object so it bounces on water (skimming stones)
To ricochet
To read quickly, skipping some detail

definitions.definition: To scrape off; to remove (something) from a surface
To clear (a liquid) from scum or substance floating or lying on it, by means of a utensil that passes just beneath the surface.

definitions.definition: To clear a liquid from (scum or substance floating or lying on it), especially the cream that floats on top of fresh milk

definitions.definition: To steal money from a business before the transaction has been recorded, thus avoiding detection.
To surreptitiously scan a payment card in order to obtain its information for fraudulent purposes.
To become coated over.
A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A liquid content (e.g. oil) which acts as a binding and drying agent in paint. (FM 55-501).
(pharmaceuticals) The main excipient (such as an oil or gel) that conveys the active ingredient of a drug.
An entity to achieve an end.
A mode or method of spiritual practice; a yana.
An animal or (rarely) a plant on which a Hindu deity rides or sits
Someone who physically resembles (looks like) someone else.

definitions.definition: A large piece of machinery that digs holes or trenches; an excavator.
A tool for digging.
A spade (playing card).
One who digs.
A gold miner, one who digs for gold.
An informal nickname for a friend; used as a term of endearment.
An Australian soldier.
One who exerts power, or has the power to act

A person who looks for work for another person
Someone who works for an intelligence agency
An active power or cause or substance; something which has the power to produce an effect

In the client-server model, the part of the system that performs information preparation and exchange on behalf of a client or server. Especially in the phrase “intelligent agent” it implies some kind of autonomous process which can communicate with other agents to perform some collective task on behalf of one or more humans.
(grammar) The participant of a situation that carries out the action in this situation, e.g. "the boy" in the sentences "The boy kicked the ball" and "The ball was kicked by the boy".
A cheat who is assisted by dishonest casino staff.
A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
A sum of money deposited at a bank and subject to withdrawal.

definitions.definition: A statement in general of reasons, causes, grounds, etc., explanatory of some event; a reason of an action to be done.

definitions.definition: A reason, grounds, consideration, motive; a person's sake.

definitions.definition: A business relationship involving the exchange of money and credit.
A record of events; recital of transactions; a relation or narrative; a report; a description.

definitions.definition: An estimate or estimation; valuation; judgment.
Importance; worth; value; esteem; judgement.
An authorization to use a service.

definitions.definition: A reckoning; computation; calculation; enumeration; a record of some reckoning.
Profit; advantage.
A place of safety.
The protection, physical and legal, afforded by such a place.
A place of protection or restraint for one or more classes of the disadvantaged, especially the mentally ill.
The act of injecting, or something that is injected.
A specimen prepared by injection.
A morphism from either one of the two components of a coproduct to that coproduct.
The act of inserting materials like concrete grout or gravel by using high pressure pumps.
The supply of additional funding to a person or a business.

definitions.definition: A relation on sets (X,Y) that associates each element of Y with at most one element of X.
The insertion of program code into an application, URL, hardware, etc.; especially when malicious or when the target is not designed for such insertion.

definitions.definition: The act of putting a spacecraft into a particular orbit, especially for changing a stable orbit into a transfer orbit, e.g. trans-lunar injection.
A function that maps distinct x in the domain to distinct y in the codomain; formally, a f: X → Y such that f(a) = f(b) implies a = b for any a, b in the domain.
(specifically) Something injected subcutaneously, intravenously, or intramuscularly by use of a syringe and a needle.
(steam engines) The act of throwing cold water into a condenser to produce a vacuum.
(steam engines) The cold water thrown into a condenser to produce a vacuum.
A bag stitched to an item of clothing, used for carrying small items.
Such a receptacle seen as housing someone's money; hence, financial resources.

definitions.definition: An indention and cavity with a net sack or similar structure (into which the balls are to be struck) at each corner and one centered on each side of a pool or snooker table.
An enclosed volume of one substance surrounded by another.

definitions.definition: An area of land surrounded by a loop of a river.

The area of the field to the side of the goal posts (four pockets in total on the field, one to each side of the goals at each end of the ground). The pocket is only a roughly defined area, extending from the behind post, at an angle, to perhaps about 30 meters out.
The region directly behind the offensive line in which the quarterback executes plays.
An area where military units are completely surrounded by enemy units.
The position held by a second defensive middle, where an advanced middle must retreat after making a touch on the attacking middle.
A large bag or sack formerly used for packing various articles, such as ginger, hops, or cowries; the pocket of wool held about 168 pounds.
A hole or space covered by a movable piece of board, as in a floor, boxing, partitions, etc.
A cavity in a rock containing a nugget of gold, or other mineral; a small body of ore contained in such a cavity.
A strip of canvas sewn upon a sail so that a batten or a light spar can placed in the interspace.
The pouch of an animal.
The ideal point where the pins are hit by the bowling ball.
A socket for receiving the base of a post, stake, etc.
A bight on a lee shore.
A small space between a tooth and the adjoining gum, formed by an abnormal separation of the two.
To put (something) into a pocket.
To cause a ball to go into one of the pockets of the table; to complete a shot.
To take and keep (something, especially money that is not one's own).

definitions.definition: To shoplift; to steal.

definitions.definition: To put up with; to bear without complaint.

An orange colour, like that of papaya flesh.
A plum.
The dried, wrinkled fruit of certain species of plum.
An old woman, especially a wrinkly one.
To become wrinkled like a dried plum, as the fingers and toes do when kept submerged in water.
A V-shaped pattern; used in architecture, and as an insignia of military or police rank, on the sleeve

One of the V-shaped markings on the surface of roads used to indicate minimum distances between vehicles.
A guillemet, either of the punctuation marks “«” or “»”, used in several languages to indicate passages of speech. Similar to typical quotation marks used in the English language such as ““” and “””.
An angle bracket, either used as a typographic or a scientific symbol.
A háček, a diacritical mark that may resemble an inverted circumflex.
To form or be formed into chevrons
A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.
The upper portion of something

That which is higher, contrasted with the lower.
A spiritual passageway through which consciousness can reach a higher dimension.
At a higher level, rank or position.
Situated on higher ground, further inland, or more northerly.
(of strata or geological time periods) younger, more recent
Of or pertaining to a secondary school.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
Set with jewels
Sharp; sour; acid.
Sharp; keen; bitter; severe.
Desirous; keen to do or obtain something.

definitions.definition: Brittle; inflexible; not ductile.
Not employing lazy evaluation; calculating results immediately, rather than deferring calculation until they are required.

definitions.definition: The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.

definitions.definition: A force that tends to oppose motion.

An underground organisation engaged in a struggle for liberation from forceful occupation; a resistance movement.

One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
A new participant in some activity; a neophyte.
Any of the poisonous umbelliferous plants, of the genera
The poison obtained from these Conium and Cicuta plants.

definitions.definition: A familiar, relatable feeling, experience, or thing.
Courage, heart, valor; also vim and vigor.

definitions.definition: (grammar) In a clause: the word or word group (usually a noun phrase) that is dealt with. In active clauses with verbs denoting an action, the subject and the actor are usually the same.

definitions.definition: An actor; one who takes action.

definitions.definition: The main topic of a paper, work of art, discussion, field of study, etc.

definitions.definition: Wished-for; desired, wanted.
Expressing a wish or longing for something.
Aspiring, or seeking advancement.

A player on the Minnesota Vikings NFL team.
A vivid, unpleasant mental image, having the characteristics of a nightmare, during wakefulness.
The act of concealing oneself and lying in wait to attack by surprise.
An attack launched from a concealed position.
The troops posted in a concealed place, for attacking by surprise; those who lie in wait.
To station in ambush with a view to surprise an enemy.

Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
A mechanism fitted to a cannon that fires it when a cord is pulled.
Literature composed in verse or language exhibiting conscious attention to patterns and rhythm.
A poet's literary production.
An artistic quality that appeals to or evokes the emotions, in any medium; something having such a quality.

To play a block shot.

definitions.definition: To wait.

Obstructed impeding general movement.

definitions.definition: Obstructed impeding total flow in a pipe, etc.

A spectator; someone looks on or watches, without becoming involved or participating.

Of a system: connected (generally electrically) to a larger network.
Available over, or delivered from, the Internet.

definitions.definition: While online; while in a running or active state, or connected to the Internet.

(rare) the ordinary or routine day or occasion
Appropriate for ordinary use, rather than for special occasions
Commonplace, ordinary
Wages, money earned, income.
Business profits.
Gains on investments; returns.

Waterproof rubberized cloth.

A nonreproductive social insect, such as ant, bee, termite, or wasp.
A thread performing one instance of a particular task within a program.

Sloth (animal).
To laze, act in a lazy manner.
Unwilling to do work or make an effort; disinclined to exertion.

definitions.definition: Causing or characterised by idleness; relaxed or leisurely.

definitions.definition: Showing a lack of effort or care.

definitions.definition: Sluggish; slow-moving.

definitions.definition: Lax:
(of a cattle brand) Turned so that (the letter) is horizontal instead of vertical.
Employing lazy evaluation; not calculating results until they are immediately required.

definitions.definition: Wicked; vicious.
One who or that which launches. A device that throws something or the person who initiates a launch.

One who has received the highest of the four minor orders in the Catholic Church, being ordained to carry the wine, water and lights at Mass.
An altar server.
An attendant, assistant or follower.
To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;—with an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.
definitions.example: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.  We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.

definitions.definition: To offer, provide, or supply, as in selling, granting, expending, with profit, or without loss or too great injury.

definitions.definition: To give forth; to supply, yield, or produce as the natural result, fruit, or issue.

definitions.definition: To establish the taxonomic classification of an organism.
To equate or make the same; to unite or combine into one.
To have a strong affinity with; to feel oneself to be modelled on or connected to.

A special piece of clothing worn by soldiers to protect them from shrapnel and grenade fragments, other non-direct fire and battlefield debris. Sometimes used informally to connote any of the various forms of a bullet-proof vest.
A pancake.

A pop-up covering an existing part of the display.

Furnishing help; giving aid; useful.

A single cluster or group of plants growing close together, and having the earth heaped up about them.

To heap or draw earth around plants.

A flat-bottomed cargo boat; mackinaw boat.
To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.

Absence of justice; unjustice.
Violation of the rights of another person or people.
